TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: izua sitsuayan marekaɫaaɫak a matjelu.
S.PHON: izua siʦuajan marəkaʎaaʎak a macəɭu.
S.FORM: izua sicuayan marekaljaaljak a matjelu.
S.PHON: izua siʦuajan maRəkaʎaaʎak a maʦəɣu.
S.TRANSL: Once upon a time there was a family of three.

S.FORM: uzai a aɫak nua kina a lumamad anan.
S.PHON: uzai a aʎak nua kina a ɭumamad anan.
S.FORM: uzai a aljak nua kina a lumamad anan.
S.PHON: uzai a aʎak nua kina a ɣumamad anan.
S.TRANSL: The mother had a child which was still a baby,

S.FORM: qau sipasevaɫ tua aɫak a tjaɫavuluvulungan a vavayan.
S.PHON: qau sipasəvaʎ tua aʎak a caʎavuɭuvuɭuŋan a vavajan.
S.FORM: qau sipasevalj tua aljak a tjaljavuluvulungan a vavayan.
S.PHON: qau sipasəvaʎ tua aʎak a ʦaʎavuɣuvuɣuŋan a vavajan.
S.TRANSL: which she gave to the oldest girl to carry on her back.

S.FORM: qau sa vaik a kina a kivurasi.
S.PHON: qau sa vaik a kina a kivurasi.
S.FORM: qau sa vaik a kina a kivurasi.
S.PHON: qau sa vaik a kina a kivuRasi.
S.TRANSL: And the mother went off to get sweet potatoes.

S.FORM: manu tsuay anga aravats, qemaung a keḍikeḍi.
S.PHON: manu ʦuaj aŋa aravaʦ, qəmauŋ a kəɖikəɖi.
S.FORM: manu cuay anga aravac, qemaung a kedrikedri.
S.PHON: manu ʦuaj aŋa aRavaʦ, qəmauŋ a kəɖikəɖi.
S.TRANSL: After a long time the baby cried.

S.FORM: qau katsuin nazua tjavulung a kaka pasa tjua kina.
S.PHON: qau kaʦuin nazua cavuɭuŋ a kaka pasa cua kina.
S.FORM: qau kacuin nazua tjavulung a kaka pasa tjua kina.
S.PHON: qau kaʦuin nazua ʦavuɣuŋ a kaka pasa ʦua kina.
S.TRANSL: So the older sister took him to their mother.

S.FORM: qau kiɫavaran a aɫak a tjaɫavuluvulungan: "sualapu ti aḍing a qemaung," ayain a kina.
S.PHON: qau kiʎavaran a aʎak a caʎavuɭuvuɭuŋan: "suaɭapu ti aɖiŋ a qəmauŋ," ajain a kina.
S.FORM: qau kiljavaran a aljak a tjaljavuluvulungan: "sualapu ti adring a qemaung," ayain a kina.
S.PHON: qau kiʎavaRan a aʎak a ʦaʎavuɣuvuɣuŋan: "suaɣapu ti aɖiŋ a qəmauŋ," ajain a kina.
S.TRANSL: The oldest child said: “Take the boy away; he’s crying,” she said to her mother.

S.FORM: ɫakua "gaɫu anan; kivurasi anan aken," aya kina.
S.PHON: ʎakua "gaʎu anan; kivurasi anan akən," aja kina.
S.FORM: ljakua "galju anan; kivurasi anan aken," aya kina.
S.PHON: ʎakua "gaʎu anan; kivuRasi anan akən," aja kina.
S.TRANSL: But the mother said: “Wait a bit; I’m still getting sweet potatoes.”

S.FORM: qemaung aravats a keḍikeḍi.
S.PHON: qəmauŋ aravaʦ a kəɖikəɖi.
S.FORM: qemaung aravac a kedrikedri.
S.PHON: qəmauŋ aRavaʦ a kəɖikəɖi.
S.TRANSL: The baby cried a lot.

S.FORM: "sualapu ti aḍing," ayain anan uta a kina.
S.PHON: "suaɭapu ti aɖiŋ," ajain anan uta a kina.
S.FORM: "sualapu ti adring," ayain anan uta a kina.
S.PHON: "suaɣapu ti aɖiŋ," ajain anan uta a kina.
S.TRANSL: “Take the boy away,” the girl said once more to her mother.

S.FORM: ɫakua ini sualapi angata.
S.PHON: ʎakua ini suaɭapi aŋata.
S.FORM: ljakua ini sualapi angata.
S.PHON: ʎakua ini suaɣapi aŋata.
S.TRANSL: But she just would not take him.

S.FORM: qau keman tua tsengeɫ; ini pakani.
S.PHON: qau kəman tua ʦəŋəʎ; ini pakani.
S.FORM: qau keman tua cengelj; ini pakani.
S.PHON: qau kəman tua ʦəŋəʎ; ini pakani.
S.TRANSL: She ate lunch, but didn’t feed the children.

S.FORM: qau kisankuya azua kakeḍian; vaik a sema qapaz.
S.PHON: qau kisankuja azua kakəɖian; vaik a səma qapaz.
S.FORM: qau kisankuya azua kakedrian; vaik a sema qapaz.
S.PHON: qau kisankuja azua kakəɖian; vaik a səma qapaz.
S.TRANSL: The children were angry, and went away to the edge of the field.

S.FORM: qau sa pedii a siaɫa katua tjalikuvaɫ tu sanpalaɫen niamadju a maḍusa.
S.PHON: qau sa pədii a siaʎa katua caɭikuvaʎ tu sanpaɭaʎən niamaɉu a maɖusa.
S.FORM: qau sa pedii a sialja katua tjalikuvalj tu sanpalaljen niamadju a madrusa.
S.PHON: qau sa pədii a siaʎa katua ʦaɣikuvaʎ tu sanpaɣaʎən niamaɉu a maɖusa.
S.TRANSL: The carrying-cloth and the baby blanket were torn up to make wings for the two of them;

S.FORM: qau kitsaquan tiamadju a milayap.
S.PHON: qau kiʦaquan tiamaɉu a miɭajap.
S.FORM: qau kicaquan tiamadju a milayap.
S.PHON: qau kiʦaquan tiamaɉu a miɣajap.
S.TRANSL: and they practised flying.

S.FORM: qau tsuay anga aravats vaiken a karim nua kina.
S.PHON: qau ʦuaj aŋa aravaʦ vaikən a karim nua kina.
S.FORM: qau cuay anga aravac vaiken a karim nua kina.
S.PHON: qau ʦuaj aŋa aRavaʦ vaikən a kaRim nua kina.
S.TRANSL: After a long time the mother went to look for them.

S.FORM: mintu kitsaquaquan a milayap.
S.PHON: mintu kiʦaquaquan a miɭajap.
S.FORM: mintu kicaquaquan a milayap.
S.PHON: mintu kiʦaquaquan a miɣajap.
S.TRANSL: And they were practising flying.

S.FORM: "aku maitazuazua mun?" ayain nua kina a kiɫavaran a kakeḍian.
S.PHON: "aku maitazuazua mun" ajain nua kina a kiʎavaran a kakəɖian.
S.FORM: "aku maitazuazua mun?" ayain nua kina a kiljavaran a kakedrian.
S.PHON: "aku maitazuazua mun" ajain nua kina a kiʎavaRan a kakəɖian.
S.TRANSL: “Why are you doing that?” said the mother to the children.

S.FORM: "vaiku a tjumaq," ayain azua kakeḍian.
S.PHON: "vaiku a cumaq," ajain azua kakəɖian.
S.FORM: "vaiku a tjumaq," ayain azua kakedrian.
S.PHON: "vaiku a ʦumaq," ajain azua kakəɖian.
S.TRANSL: “Go home!” the children were told.

S.FORM: ɫakua ini kisalu a vaik.
S.PHON: ʎakua ini kisaɭu a vaik.
S.FORM: ljakua ini kisalu a vaik.
S.PHON: ʎakua ini kisaɣu a vaik.
S.TRANSL: But they wouldn’t go.

S.FORM: "muri masan qayaqayam amen," aya kiɫavaran tua kina.
S.PHON: "muri masan qajaqajam amən," aja kiʎavaran tua kina.
S.FORM: "muri masan qayaqayam amen," aya kiljavaran tua kina.
S.PHON: "muRi masan qajaqajam amən," aja kiʎavaRan tua kina.
S.TRANSL: “We’re going to become birds,” they said to their mother.

S.FORM: qau kagarangan nua kina.
S.PHON: qau kagaraŋan nua kina.
S.FORM: qau kagarangan nua kina.
S.PHON: qau kagaRaŋan nua kina.
S.TRANSL: The mother scolded them.

S.FORM: milayap a mavilad a sema tsemtsemel.
S.PHON: miɭajap a maviɭad a səma ʦəmʦəməɭ.
S.FORM: milayap a mavilad a sema cemcemel.
S.PHON: miɣajap a maviɣad a səma ʦəmʦəməɣ.
S.TRANSL: They flew off to the woods.

S.FORM: qau azua kakeḍian maɫavar tiamadju: "tisun layapu a maka kaledep; tiaken a maka maza i katsedas," ayain nua tjavulung a tjaɫaɫak.
S.PHON: qau azua kakəɖian maʎavar tiamaɉu: "tisun ɭajapu a maka kaɭədəp; tiakən a maka maza i kaʦədas," ajain nua cavuɭuŋ a caʎaʎak.
S.FORM: qau azua kakedrian maljavar tiamadju: "tisun layapu a maka kaledep; tiaken a maka maza i kacedas," ayain nua tjavulung a tjaljaljak.
S.PHON: qau azua kakəɖian maʎavaR tiamaɉu: "tisun ɣajapu a maka kaɣədəp; tiakən a maka maza i kaʦədas," ajain nua ʦavuɣuŋ a ʦaʎaʎak.
S.TRANSL: The children discussed together: “You fly in the west; I’ll be here in the east,” said the older one to the younger one.

S.FORM: qau muri mavaday anga tiamadju; matjatjuktjuk.
S.PHON: qau muri mavadaj aŋa tiamaɉu; macacukcuk.
S.FORM: qau muri mavaday anga tiamadju; matjatjuktjuk.
S.PHON: qau muRi mavadaj aŋa tiamaɉu; maʦaʦukʦuk.
S.TRANSL: They were going to part, and they touched beaks.

S.FORM: qau sa kaɫavar: "nu metsevung itjen, uɫa quḍaquḍasan anga," aya tiamadju.
S.PHON: qau sa kaʎavar: "nu məʦəvuŋ icən, uʎa quɖaquɖasan aŋa," aja tiamaɉu.
S.FORM: qau sa kaljavar: "nu mecevung itjen, ulja qudraqudrasan anga," aya tiamadju.
S.PHON: qau sa kaʎavaR: "nu məʦəvuŋ iʦən, uʎa quɖaquɖasan aŋa," aja tiamaɉu.
S.TRANSL: And they said to one another: “Let’s be white-haired when we meet again,” they said.

S.FORM: qau milayap anga tiamadju a mavadai a maḍusa.
S.PHON: qau miɭajap aŋa tiamaɉu a mavadai a maɖusa.
S.FORM: qau milayap anga tiamadju a mavadai a madrusa.
S.PHON: qau miɣajap aŋa tiamaɉu a mavadai a maɖusa.
S.TRANSL: And the two of them flew off and parted.

S.FORM: qau metsevung tiamadju ka quḍaquḍasan anga tiamadju a qayaqayam.
S.PHON: qau məʦəvuŋ tiamaɉu ka quɖaquɖasan aŋa tiamaɉu a qajaqajam.
S.FORM: qau mecevung tiamadju ka qudraqudrasan anga tiamadju a qayaqayam.
S.PHON: qau məʦəvuŋ tiamaɉu ka quɖaquɖasan aŋa tiamaɉu a qajaqajam.
S.TRANSL: They met again when they were white-haired birds.

S.FORM: qau maɫavar anan tiamadju uta: "tisun ngangay aya-u anga.
S.PHON: qau maʎavar anan tiamaɉu uta: "tisun ŋaŋaj aja-u aŋa.
S.FORM: qau maljavar anan tiamadju uta: "tisun ngangay aya-u anga.
S.PHON: qau maʎavaR anan tiamaɉu uta: "tisun ŋaŋaj aja-u aŋa.
S.TRANSL: And they talked to one another again: “You say ‘ngangay’,

S.FORM: tiaken tjugelui ayanga aken nu zemaing," ayain a tjaɫaɫak.
S.PHON: tiakən cugəɭui ajaŋa akən nu zəmaiŋ," ajain a caʎaʎak.
S.FORM: tiaken tjugelui ayanga aken nu zemaing," ayain a tjaljaljak.
S.PHON: tiakən ʦugəɣui ajaŋa akən nu zəmaiŋ," ajain a ʦaʎaʎak.
S.TRANSL: and I’ll say ‘tjugelui’ when we call,” the younger one was told.

S.FORM: avan sika uzai anga nua tjugelui katua ngangay tutsu.
S.PHON: avan sika uzai aŋa nua cugəɭui katua ŋaŋaj tuʦu.
S.FORM: avan sika uzai anga nua tjugelui katua ngangay tucu.
S.PHON: avan sika uzai aŋa nua ʦugəɣui katua ŋaŋaj tuʦu.
S.TRANSL: And that’s why there are the ngangay (white owl) and tjugelui (brown owl) to this day.
